perturbation.original_text: 3. FEES 3.1 The Company will pay the Consultant basic remuneration for its services in the sum of $10,000 per month (the "Consulting Fee") commencing on the 1st day of February, 2020, and payable on the last day of each month thereafter up to and including the 31st day of January, 2025, together with any such increments thereto as the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors of the Company may from time to time determine. In addition, the Company will pay to the Consultant all reasonable expenses of the Consultant as agreed to from time to time which are incurred by the Consultant in delivery of the Services, based on monthly invoices submitted to the Company, including copies of all paid receipts; plus harmonized sales taxes or goods and services taxes, as the case may be, in addition to the Consulting Fees, which taxes will be remitted by the Consultant to the Canada Revenue Agency.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. FEES 3.1 The Company will pay the Consultant basic remuneration for its services in the sum of $10,000 per month (the "Expense Reimbursement") commencing on the 1st day of February, 2020, and payable on the last day of each month thereafter up to and including the 31st day of January, 2025, together with any such increments thereto as the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors of the Company may from time to time determine. In addition, the Company will pay to the Consultant all reasonable expenses of the Consultant as agreed to from time to time which are incurred by the Consultant in delivery of the Services, based on monthly invoices submitted to the Company, including copies of all paid receipts; plus harmonized sales taxes or goods and services taxes, as the case may be, in addition to the Consulting Fees, which taxes will be remitted by the Consultant to the Canada Revenue Agency.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Consulting Fee" to "Expense Reimbursement" in Section 3.1 introduces a contradiction because later in the same section, there is already a clause discussing payment for "all reasonable expenses." Changing the definition of the monthly payment from "Consulting Fee" to "Expense Reimbursement" creates confusion because the phrase 'in addition to the Consulting Fees, which taxes will be remitted by the Consultant to the Canada Revenue Agency.' Now it is unclear what the consulting fees are because it was previusly defined, but has now been changed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. FEES 3.1 The Company will pay the Consultant basic remuneration for its services in the sum of $10,000 per month (the "Expense Reimbursement") commencing on the 1st day of February, 2020, and payable on the last day of each month thereafter up to and including the 31st day of January, 2025, together with any such increments thereto as the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors of the Company may from time to time determine. In addition, the Company will pay to the Consultant all reasonable expenses of the Consultant as agreed to from time to time which are incurred by the Consultant in delivery of the Services, based on monthly invoices submitted to the Company, including copies of all paid receipts; plus harmonized sales taxes or goods and services taxes, as the case may be, in addition to the Consulting Fees, which taxes will be remitted by the Consultant to the Canada Revenue Agency.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Consulting Fee' to 'Expense Reimbursement' introduces a contradiction. While initially defining the $10,000 payment as 'Expense Reimbursement,' the clause later refers to 'Consulting Fees' when discussing taxes. This creates a clear contradiction and ambiguity about what the consulting fees actually are, given the change, and if the expense reimbursement is the same as the consulting fee. This satisfies the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 4. TERM AND RENEWAL 4.1 During the term of this Agreement, the Consultant shall provide its Services to the Company through its Principal, and the Consultant shall ensure that the Principal will be available to provide such Services to the Company in a timely manner.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. TERM AND RENEWAL 4.1 During the term of this Agreement, the Consultant shall provide its Services to the Company through its Lead Technician, and the Consultant shall ensure that the Lead Technician will be available to provide such Services to the Company in a timely manner.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Principal" to "Lead Technician" in Section 4.1, where the Principal is defined as David Wolfin. This creates an in-text contradiction as the schedule “A” makes it clear that the consultant shall serve as president and chief executive officer. This change affects clause 4.2, as it is unclear who the five year term applies to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 The term of this Agreement is for a period of five (5) years (the "Term") commencing on the Effective Date and, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the termination provisions of this Agreement, ending on January 31, 2025.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Principal" to "Lead Technician" in Section 4.1 creates a contradiction because the Principal is explicitly defined as David Wolfin (the president and CEO), while the "Lead Technician" is undefined, making it unclear who the five-year term in Section 4.2 applies to. This introduces ambiguity and inconsistency, creating a strong in-text contradiction based on misaligned terminology. It makes the agreement unclear and potentially unenforceable regarding the term's applicability, thereby fulfilling the definition of misaligned terminology and creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Duties and Responsibilities: The Executive shall serve the Company as an executive officer in the position of President and Chief Executive Officer. The Executive shall report to the Board and shall undertake and perform the following duties and responsibilities: · actively engage with the Board to ensure that the initiatives of the management team are aligned with the strategic direction and objectives for the Company that have been established by the Board; · provide overall direction for the Company in order for it to implement agreed strategies in order to meet Company goals and objectives; · provide shareholder and investor communication and manage key investment banking and institutional relationships; · make decisions in line with organizational goals, leading to desired results, and will be responsible and accountable for results; · create and sustain the organizational culture and environment needed to achieve objectives and results and recruit and retain a high-performance operating team; · oversee the implementation and monitoring of internal controls, reporting compliance obligations, sign off on CEO Certificates for the interim and annual financial statements and setting environmental protection policies; and · such other duties and responsibilities as may be assigned or vested in him by the Board from time to time and which are consistent with the duties and responsibilities of a President and Chief Executive Officer. The Executive agrees, during the continuance of his employment, to devote sufficient working time, services, skill and ability to such employment and to serve at all times with loyalty and honesty in the best interests of the Company. The Executive acknowledges that the position of President and CEO will involve significant travel for business development and for investor relations.
perturbation.changed_text: Duties and Responsibilities: The Consultant shall serve the Company as an executive officer in the position of President and Chief Executive Officer. The Consultant shall report to the Board and shall undertake and perform the following duties and responsibilities: · actively engage with the Board to ensure that the initiatives of the management team are aligned with the strategic direction and objectives for the Company that have been established by the Board; · provide overall direction for the Company in order for it to implement agreed strategies in order to meet Company goals and objectives; · provide shareholder and investor communication and manage key investment banking and institutional relationships; · make decisions in line with organizational goals, leading to desired results, and will be responsible and accountable for results; · create and sustain the organizational culture and environment needed to achieve objectives and results and recruit and retain a high-performance operating team; · oversee the implementation and monitoring of internal controls, reporting compliance obligations, sign off on CEO Certificates for the interim and annual financial statements and setting environmental protection policies; and · such other duties and responsibilities as may be assigned or vested in him by the Board from time to time and which are consistent with the duties and responsibilities of a President and Chief Executive Officer. The Executive agrees, during the continuance of his employment, to devote sufficient working time, services, skill and ability to such employment and to serve at all times with loyalty and honesty in the best interests of the Company. The Executive acknowledges that the position of President and CEO will involve significant travel for business development and for investor relations.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "The Executive shall serve the Company as an executive officer in the position of President and Chief Executive Officer" to "The Consultant shall serve the Company as an executive officer in the position of President and Chief Executive Officer" in Schedule A introduces a conflict. Later in the schedule, the text refers to "The Executive agrees, during the continuance of his employment, to devote sufficient working time..." This creates ambiguity because the document refers to the 'Executive' as if it's someone different from the 'Consultant', the original text does not create this ambiguity as the executive is not directly linked to the Consultant. This means, the 'Consultant' may not be an employee of the company, however the 'Executive' is. The changed text introduces an ambiguity in Schedule A about how that will be handled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Executive agrees, during the continuance of his employment, to devote sufficient working time, services, skill and ability to such employment and to serve at all times with loyalty and honesty in the best interests of the Company. The Executive acknowledges that the position of President and CEO will involve significant travel for business development and for investor relations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by referring to the individual as a 'Consultant' while the contradicted text refers to the same individual as 'The Executive' and assumes an employment relationship (e.g., 'during the continuance of his employment'). This creates a conflict because consultants are not necessarily employees, leading to uncertainty about whether the 'Consultant' is bound by the employment obligations outlined in the contradicted text. The original text removes this ambiguity because both the changed and contradicted sections refer to the same individual as the 'Executive'.
